# Figure description: Карта_аномального_магнитного_поля.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Карта_аномального_магнитного_поля.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **magnetic anomaly isoline map** (карта аномального магнитного поля), specifically a ΔTa (anomalous total magnetic field intensity) map compiled at a scale of **1:500,000**. It is a regional geophysical map showing contoured isolines of magnetic field intensity across a large multi-sheet area of northern Kazakhstan.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a large composite area assembled from multiple 1:500,000 and 1:200,000 map sheets across two nomenclature zones:

- **N-42 sheets:** 137-В,Г; 138-В,Г; 126-В,Г; 139; 140; 128-Г; 129-В,Г; 141
- **M-42 sheets:** 3-В,Г; 4; 5; 6; 7-А,Б; 8-А,Б; 9; 15; 16; 17; 18; 26; 27; 28; 29; 38; 39; 40; 41; 51; 52; 53; 54; 64-А Б; 65; 66

This corresponds to a broad region of **northern Kazakhstan** (Тениз area, likely the Tengiz/Tenizy basin region), as confirmed by the report attribution to the **Западно-Тенизская партия (West-Tenizy Party), 1978–1984**. The drawing is labeled **Чертёж № 45** (Drawing No. 45). A small **index map (схема расположения листов)** is shown in the upper right, indicating the spatial arrangement of the individual map sheets.

---

## Key Content

**Isoline Pattern and Anomalies:**
The map displays contoured ΔTa isolines at an **interval of 8 nT (8 мТл or мГ)**, with the following symbology (from the legend):

- **Positive isolines** — shown as solid lines
- **Zero isolines** — shown as dashed lines
- **Negative isolines** — shown as dashed lines with a different pattern

**Colour coding** reflects magnetic field intensity:
- **Pink/rose shading** — broad areas of **weakly positive to moderate positive** magnetic anomaly (background regional field)
- **Blue/cyan shading** — areas of **strongly positive (high-intensity) anomalies**, concentrated in discrete closures
- **White/uncoloured areas** — near-zero or weakly negative field zones

**Intensity scale (шкала интенсивности ΔTa):** The scale bar runs from strongly negative (менее -15) through zero to strongly positive values (up to 20+ nT), with blue representing the highest positive values and white/blank representing near-zero.

**Anomaly distribution:**
- Several **pronounced, tightly closed positive anomalies** (blue, with multiple concentric contours) are visible in the **northeastern and east-central portions** of the map, suggesting localized highly magnetic bodies at depth — potentially mafic/ultramafic intrusions, magnetite-bearing iron formations, or basement highs.
- The **central and western portions** show broad, diffuse, weakly positive (pink) regional magnetic background with gentle, open contour patterns typical of a relatively uniform crystalline basement at greater depth or sedimentary cover with low magnetic susceptibility.
- The **southwestern corner** shows another cluster of tightly closed anomalies (both positive and negative), indicating a structurally complex zone.
- Linear magnetic gradients in several areas likely reflect **faults or lithological boundaries** in the crystalline basement.
- The number **50** is annotated in blue near a prominent anomaly in the north-central area, likely a contour value or anomaly identifier.

## Research Value

This regional ΔTa map provides a **first-order characterization of basement magnetic susceptibility and structural architecture** across a ~200,000 km² area of northern Kazakhstan, allowing geologists to identify zones of magnetic crystalline basement highs, major fault systems, and the boundaries between magnetically contrasting
